title: Contract Tests, Fault Injection, Deployment, Upgrade, and Recovery · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for contract tests, fault injection, deployment, upgrade, and recovery and requires the smallest defensible tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for tested, recoverable RabbitMQ production release. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers.
Inject return, nack, close, heartbeat loss, blocked connection, duplicate, poison, dependency timeout, majority loss, restart, and stale callback failures deterministically.
Roll out schema, topology, publisher, consumer, policy, and broker changes in a compatibility-safe order with shadow evidence and rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Production Defense
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 5350 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on contracts, broker faults, compatible rollout, rollback, and supported upgrade path; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Production Defense.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend architecture, contracts, live drills, capacity, security, dashboards, runbooks, rollback, recovery objectives, residual risks, and ownership before release.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Production Defense, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Passing unit tests and a local Docker demo proves production messaging readiness.
